item: Traditional cultural practices should be preserved even when they conflict with modern values.

flip_turn.quote: Saya sepenuhnya setuju dengan pandangan Anda tentang pentingnya adaptasi tradisi untuk mencerminkan nilai modern, terutama ketika ia melanggar hak dasar.

notes: Both agents opened from nearly identical 'balance' frames (neither strongly pro-preservation), so initial tension was low. Agent A's probe started at P=0.500 (digit-4 dominated, P(4)=0.995) — no strong commitment to preservation from turn 1. Agent B immediately affirmed A's framing in turn 2 ('The Indonesian perspective emphasizes balance, which I support'), leaving nothing to rebut. Agent A progressively adopted B's civil-rights framing: turn 3 opens 'saya setuju', turn 5 opens 'saya sepenuhnya setuju' (I fully agree) — the probe drops from 0.483 to 0.376 at this turn, the largest single-turn shift in the debate. Agent B held a consistent US civil-rights / legal-protections position throughout (P moves only 0.481→0.468). Language-holding: clean — A in Indonesian throughout (turns 1,3,5), B in English throughout (turns 2,4,6), no code-switching detected. Engagement scores are moderate (4) for both: agents reference each other's specific points, but mostly confirmatory rather than adversarial — no genuine rebuttal of an opposed position because both started from compatible 'balance' frames. The pattern of asymmetric drift (A moves toward B, B stays fixed) is consistent with all prior phase2_iter1 transcripts.
